title: How to Read Code Before Writing It
story_hook: Your crush's Instagram feed has 1 million posts. You don't write code first - you read and trace how others did it. Today you become a code detective.

content.story: Your crush's Instagram feed has 1 million posts. You don't write code first - you read and trace how others did it. Today you become a code detective.

content.title: Learn How To Read Code
content.focus_note: Watch the tracing technique. Pause and predict before running.

content.doc_md:
Read line-by-line. Predict output before running.

Pro tip: use pen and paper.

visualizer.task: Trace execution with a line highlighter.
visualizer.notes: Follow the variable values at each line.

practice_problems.prompt: Predict output: a=3; b=a*2; a=b+1; print(a)
Predict output: for i in range(3): print(i)
Predict output: n=4; if n>2: print('yes') else: print('no')

quiz_questions.question: What should you do before running code you didn't write?
quiz_questions.explanation: Prediction builds deep understanding and catches mistakes early.

quiz_questions.question: Why use pen and paper while tracing?
quiz_questions.explanation: Writing states helps you follow the execution flow.

quiz_questions.question: Reading code well helps you learn
quiz_questions.explanation: Reading and tracing builds mental models quickly.

content.task_prompt: Trace a friend's code line-by-line and explain the output before running it.

content.connection_map: Clear algorithms make code easier to read.
recall_questions: What is the first thing you do when you see unfamiliar code?
How will this help you in interviews?
content.identity_affirmation: You are a coder who reads before writing.
